---
title: Identifying enriched motifs in non-homologous UBC-upregulated genes
date: 2023-02-15
metadata-files: 
  - _metadata.yml
  - ../_quarto.yml
---

## Goal of this analysis

Which TF motifs are enriched in the genes that are upregulated in the non-homologous UBCs?

Using the list of genes from 2023-02-05, the genes will be filtered by

- (nominal) *p*-value < 0.05, and
- log~2~FC < 0

to identify the genes that are upregulated in non-homologous UBCs.

From the upregulated genes, the promoter sequences will be identified and enriched motifs in those sequences will be identified using the `runAme` function from the `memes` package.


## Files and directories

### Code

See `<human Aldinger software directory>/​run_memes.R` and `<utilities>/​get_promoter_seqs.R`.

### Output

See `<human Aldinger results directory/​20230215>` for figures, CSV, and log files.


## Results

![Top 30 enriched motifs in the non-homologous UBC genes.](/CBL_scRNAseq/results/human/Aldinger/20230215/ame_heatmap_nonhomol.png){#fig-ame_heatmap}

@fig-ame_heatmap shows the top 30 enriched TF-binding motifs that were identified in genes that were upregulated in non-homologous UBCs. The full list of TFs can be found in `enriched_motifs_nonhomol.csv`.

Some notable transcription factors that were identified include: PRDM6, PRDM1, MYCN, SOX2, FOXP2, RUNX2, and RUNX1.

The next step is to determine how much overlap there is between these TFs and the genes that have been found to be mutated in MB.
